title: Upon A Child. An Epitaph.
content:
But born, and like a short delight,
    I glided by my parents' sight.
    That done, the harder fates denied
    My longer stay, and so I died.
    If, pitying my sad parents' tears,
    You'll spill a tear or two with theirs,
    And with some flowers my grave bestrew,
    Love and they'll thank you for't. Adieu.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon A Child. An Epitaph.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
